ground_truth.title: Suggestions for a beginner mtb
ground_truth.author: Ejp55230
ground_truth.main_content:
Ejp55230
Jun 2, 2024

Im looking to get into mountain biking ive been trying to do some research on which bike to get. I want to get a good intro level bike. I dont want to waste money getting something cheap that would break or give me problems to where id end up buying another bike anyway. Want something decent and worth the money. So far im thinking either a trek marlin or a cannondale trail...any recommendations welcome.. thanks in advance!!!!!

eb1888
Jun 3, 2024

Unfortunately, good bikes with an air fork are going to seem unusually expensive to you.
Trail riding is tough on the bike. It takes a good frame and suspension to keep riding trails from beating you up.
You'll save the money you're talking about by buying a decent bike to start. It's going to cost more than you think.
This Ibis DV9 is one of the best bikes from 2023 according to YouTube Hardtail Party.
You'd keep it for years.

wolfmw
Jun 3, 2024

Trek Marlin and Cannondale Trail are both decent entry level bikes, I actually have one of each for my kids.

But we can't help much without more info about your experience level, where you want to ride, budget, what kind of shops are available nearby, etc.

Ejp55230
Jun 3, 2024

wolfmw said:
Trek Marlin and Cannondale Trail are both decent entry level bikes, I actually have one of each for my kids.

But we can't help much without more info about your experience level, where you want to ride, budget, what kind of shops are available nearby, etc.

I am a beginner ive been riding trails now for a long time....but havent done any mountain biking im going to start learning around pittsburgh theres a ton of trails around here nothing extreme. From what ive looked into, alot of it is beginner friendly which is good for me. I just would like to get something reliable and worth the money to atleast get started....id like to stay around 500 to 1000 range.....theres a bunch of shops around me trek, rei, pro bike and run......each place i go to just tries to sell there entry level wether it be the marlin or the trail which is why those 2 were my main choices so far. Just wanted a different opinion then a bike from said store...ive been leaning more towards the marlin

lvdukerider
Jun 4, 2024

The Marlin is a good choice, nothing spectacular, just a good solid bike that's upgradeable if you choose. Plus there's usually a Trek store close by so parts, service, and warranty shouldn't be a problem.

nwberm
Jun 5, 2024

You can look at the Salsa Timberjack at REI which is a good trail hardtail.

Another option that is also good but cheaper is the Salsa Rangefinder
